title: ononis alopecuroides subsp. salzmanniana
intro: Ononis alopecuroides subsp. salzmanniana, also known as Salzmann's restharrow, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the legume family, native to the Iberian Peninsula. The plant is believed to have medicinal properties, specifically its root and stem, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Ononis alopecuroides subsp. salzmanniana include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. Traditionally, the plant has been used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are still used today in some folk medicine practices.
